canvas.placeholder: Canvas — React Flow and graph editing come in phase 4 (see doc/DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md).
canvas.onlyOneStart: This graph already has a start node.

structure.noStart: No start node (runner expects a single start).
structure.startHasIncoming: Start node {{id}} has incoming edges (should have none).

inspector.heading: Inspector
inspector.hint: Click a node or edge. Drag between handles to connect. Delete/Backspace removes the selection. Right-click canvas adds a node. Inspector: data or edge condition.
inspector.label: Title
inspector.invalidDataJson: Data must be a JSON object.
inspector.edgeConditionHint: Runtime picks the first edge with no condition or a true condition (see Python runner).
inspector.openGraphRefNeedWorkspace: Link a project folder so nested graphs can be opened from graphs/.

warnings.multipleUnconditional: Node {{sourceId}}: more than one outgoing edge with no condition (runner uses JSON order).

console.heading: Console
console.stub: Run log / NDJSON stream from the Python runner will appear here.

errors.invalidGraphJson: Invalid graph file: need a JSON object with "nodes" and "edges" arrays.
errors.readJsonFailed: Could not read or parse the file as JSON.

workspace.linkedHint: Project folder is linked; graphs/ is used for Save and autosave.
workspace.notSupported: Folder linking needs a Chromium-based browser (File System Access API). Use Save to download otherwise.
workspace.linkFailed: Could not open the project folder or create graphs/.
workspace.writeFailed: Could not write the graph file.
workspace.duplicateGraphId: Another file already uses this graphId: {{file}}
workspace.needLinkForGraphRef: Link the project folder first to open a nested graph from graphs/.
workspace.graphNotInIndex: No graph file with graphId {{id}} was found in graphs/ (rescan after adding files).
